- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

.PHONY: help install dev dev-api dev-web kill-ports seed \
	db-up db-down db-reset db-logs \
	test test-py test-js format format-check python-format-check lint architecture \
	typecheck verify build clean

help:
--
	@echo "  dev       — db-up + API + Web (:$(API_PORT) / :$(WEB_PORT))"
	@echo "  verify    — format-check, lint, architecture, tc, pytest, vite build"

install:
	$(PNPM) install
	cd $(APP_DIR) && $(PYTHON) -m venv .venv && .venv/bin/pip install --upgrade pip && .venv/bin/pip install -e ".[dev]"

db-up:
	docker compose -f infra/docker-compose.yml up -d
	@echo "Postgres localhost:5545  Redis localhost:6392  MinIO http://localhost:9120"

--
	    pids=$$(lsof -ti :$$p 2>/dev/null); \
	    [ -n "$$pids" ] && kill -9 $$pids 2>/dev/null || true; \
	  done; \
	  pkill -9 -f "vite.*$$WS_TAG"     2>/dev/null || true; \
	  pkill -9 -f "uvicorn.*$$WS_TAG"   2>/dev/null || true; \
	  pkill -9 -f "concurrently.*$$WS_TAG" 2>/dev/null || true; \
	  pkill -9 -f "turbo run dev"       2>/dev/null || true; \
	  busy=""; \
	  for p in $$PORTS; do \
	    lsof -ti :$$p >/dev/null 2>&1 && busy="$$busy $$p"; \
	  done; \
	  [ -z "$$busy" ] && exit 0; \
	  sleep 0.5; \
	done; \
	echo "kill-ports: still in use after retries:$$busy" >&2; \
--
dev: kill-ports seed
else
dev: db-up kill-ports seed
endif
	@echo "→ API   http://127.0.0.1:$(API_PORT)/api/health"
	@echo "→ Web   http://127.0.0.1:$(WEB_PORT)"
	$(PNPM) -w exec concurrently -k -n api,web -c blue,magenta \
	  "$(MAKE) dev-api" \
	  "$(MAKE) dev-web"

dev-api:
	cd $(APP_DIR) && PYTHONPATH=. .venv/bin/python -m uvicorn bim_ai.main:app --host 127.0.0.1 --port $(API_PORT) --reload

dev-web:
	API_PORT=$(API_PORT) $(PNPM) --filter @bim-ai/web dev --port $(WEB_PORT) --host 127.0.0.1 --strictPort

seed:
	cd $(APP_DIR) && PYTHONPATH=. .venv/bin/python scripts/seed.py

test: test-py test-js

# bim-ai monorepo Makefile — mirrors collaboration-ai ergonomics.

_HOF_OS_IMPORT_DB := $(DATABASE_URL)
_HOF_OS_IMPORT_JWT := $(HOF_SUBAPP_JWT_SECRET)
ifneq ($(and $(strip $(_HOF_OS_IMPORT_JWT)),$(strip $(_HOF_OS_IMPORT_DB))),)
DATABASE_URL := $(_HOF_OS_IMPORT_DB)
HOF_SUBAPP_JWT_SECRET := $(_HOF_OS_IMPORT_JWT)
HOFOS_SUBAPP_NATIVE := 1
endif

PYTHON ?= python3.13
APP_DIR := app
PNPM := pnpm

# Local suite port allocation:
#   2000 → bim-ai web (:8500 API)  <-- this repo (avoids 3xxx used by sister apps)
WEB_PORT ?= 2000
API_PORT ?= 8500

.PHONY: help install dev dev-api dev-web kill-ports seed \
	db-up db-down db-reset db-logs \
	test test-py test-js format format-check python-format-check lint architecture \
	typecheck verify build clean

help:
	@echo "bim-ai Makefile"
	@echo "  install   — pnpm + Python venv"
	@echo "  dev       — db-up + API + Web (:$(API_PORT) / :$(WEB_PORT))"
	@echo "  verify    — format-check, lint, architecture, tc, pytest, vite build"

install:
	$(PNPM) install
	cd $(APP_DIR) && $(PYTHON) -m venv .venv && .venv/bin/pip install --upgrade pip && .venv/bin/pip install -e ".[dev]"

db-up:
	docker compose -f infra/docker-compose.yml up -d
	@echo "Postgres localhost:5545  Redis localhost:6392  MinIO http://localhost:9120"

db-down:
	docker compose -f infra/docker-compose.yml down

	@PORTS="$(API_PORT) $(WEB_PORT)"; \

	$(PNPM) -w turbo test

aiTitle: Apply conservative CSS design system to project

content.text:
Done. Here's the full picture:

**What was already there** — the wiring was complete: `index.css` → `@bim-ai-design-system.css` (Vite alias) → `conservative.css` or `default.css` depending on `VITE_DESIGN_SYSTEM`. The design tokens package already had the full slate/navy palette with Source Serif 4.

**What was missing** — the Makefile's `dev-web` target never forwarded the env var, so `VITE_DESIGN_SYSTEM` was always unset (defaulting to `default`). And the font wasn't being loaded.

**What I changed:**
1. `Makefile` — added `DESIGN_SYSTEM ?= default` variable and forwards it as `VITE_DESIGN_SYSTEM=$(DESIGN_SYSTEM)` in `dev-web`
2. `packages/web/src/design-systems/conservative.css` — added Google Fonts import for Source Serif 4

**To use it:**
```bash
DESIGN_SYSTEM=conservative make dev
```

Or for just the web process:
```bash
DESIGN_SYSTEM=conservative make dev-web
```

- **Face labels definition**: Line 53-60 (defines FRONT, BACK, LEFT, RIGHT, TOP, BOTTOM faces)

- **Compass subcomponent**: Line 190-204 (displays cardinal direction N/E/S/W)

- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/packages/web/src/viewport/viewCubeAlignment.ts` (pure math module for ViewCube alignment logic)

  - Face azimuth constants: Line 49-56

### 2. **"Walk: OFF" and "Section box: OFF" Overlay Labels**

- **File**: `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/packages/web/src/Viewport.tsx`

  - Label text "Walk: ON/OFF": Line 1068
- **Section box button**: Line 1070-1082
  - Label text "Section box: ON/OFF": Line 1081

- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/packages/web/src/viewport/sectionBox.ts`
  - SectionBox class: Line 38-117
  - Summary method (produces readable dimensions): Line 82-87

### 3. **Canvas Overlay/HUD Container**

- **File**: `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/packages/web/src/Viewport.tsx`
- **Main canvas container**: Line 1035-1095
- **ViewCube overlay container**: Line 1048-1054 (top-right, z-20, absolute positioned)
- **Walk/Section box toolbar container**: Line 1056-1091 (bottom-left, z-20, absolute positioned, flex column)
- **Canvas mount point**: Line 1093 (Three.js render target)

### 4. **OrbitViewpointPersistedHud Component** (saved viewpoint metadata overlay)

- **File**: `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/packages/web/src/OrbitViewpointPersistedHud.tsx`
- **Main component**: Line 44-233
- **HUD container**: Line 134-136 (bottom-right, z-10, absolute positioned)
- **Read-only display mode**: Line 145-165
- **Editable mode**: Line 166-224

- **Main component**: Line 35-118

### Layout Architecture Summary

The canvas overlay uses a nested absolute positioning model:

1. **Main container** (Viewport.tsx, line 1035-1038): `relative h-full w-full overflow-hidden bg-background`

content.text:
Good — no code references `hof-*` tokens directly, so those only affect components if they happen to use them and they're fine as extras.

The fix is complete. Here's what was wrong and what changed:

**Root cause**: `tokens-conservative.css` only defines colour palette + font-family tokens. It never defined the structural tokens that the entire UI depends on:
- `--shell-topbar-height / -statusbar-height / -rail-left-width / -rail-right-width` → the AppShell CSS grid had zero-size rows/columns, collapsing all absolute-positioned overlays to the top
- `--radius-pill`, `--elev-*`, `--motion-*`, `--ease-*`, `--text-*` scale → broken radii, shadows, and animations throughout

**Fix in `packages/web/src/design-systems/conservative.css`**: now imports in order:
1. `tokens-default.css` — all structural tokens (shell grid, spacing, radius, motion, elevation, typography scale)
2. `tokens-dark.css` — dark-mode structural overrides
3. `@bim-ai/design-tokens/conservative.css` — overrides only the colour palette + font-family on top

Also added the missing `margin: 0; padding: 0` that `default.css` had. Conservative palette wins on colour/font; default provides everything else. Any future design system you add just needs to follow the same pattern: import the default base first, then override.

# bim-ai monorepo Makefile — mirrors collaboration-ai ergonomics.

_HOF_OS_IMPORT_DB := $(DATABASE_URL)
_HOF_OS_IMPORT_JWT := $(HOF_SUBAPP_JWT_SECRET)

	@echo "bim-ai Makefile"
	@echo "  install   — pnpm + Python venv"
	@echo "  dev       — db-up + API + Web (:$(API_PORT) / :$(WEB_PORT)); DESIGN_SYSTEM=conservative make dev to use conservative theme"

install:
	$(PNPM) install
	cd $(APP_DIR) && $(PYTHON) -m venv .venv && .venv/bin/pip install --upgrade pip && .venv/bin/pip install -e ".[dev]"

db-up:
	docker compose -f infra/docker-compose.yml up -d
	@echo "Postgres localhost:5545  Redis localhost:6392  MinIO http://localhost:9120"

	docker compose -f infra/docker-compose.yml down

	$(MAKE) db-up

	docker compose -f infra/docker-compose.yml logs -f --tail=100

kill-ports:
	@PORTS="$(API_PORT) $(WEB_PORT)"; \
	WS_TAG="$(CURDIR)"; \
	for _ in 1 2 3 4 5 6; do \
	  for p in $$PORTS; do \
	    pids=$$(lsof -ti :$$p 2>/dev/null); \
	    [ -n "$$pids" ] && kill -9 $$pids 2>/dev/null || true; \
	  done; \
	  pkill -9 -f "vite.*$$WS_TAG"     2>/dev/null || true; \
	  pkill -9 -f "uvicorn.*$$WS_TAG"   2>/dev/null || true; \
	  pkill -9 -f "concurrently.*$$WS_TAG" 2>/dev/null || true; \
	  pkill -9 -f "turbo run dev"       2>/dev/null || true; \
	  busy=""; \
	  for p in $$PORTS; do \
	    lsof -ti :$$p >/dev/null 2>&1 && busy="$$busy $$p"; \
	  done; \
	  [ -z "$$busy" ] && exit 0; \
	  sleep 0.5; \
	done; \
	echo "kill-ports: still in use after retries:$$busy" >&2; \
	exit 1

ifeq ($(HOFOS_SUBAPP_NATIVE),1)
dev: kill-ports seed
else
dev: db-up kill-ports seed
endif
	@echo "→ API   http://127.0.0.1:$(API_PORT)/api/health"

	$(PNPM) -w turbo test

format:

content.text:
Found it. The bug is a two-effect architecture problem:

- **Effect 1** `[theme]`: tears down/rebuilds the renderer and creates a fresh empty `rootGroupRef.current`
- **Effect 2** `[elementsById, selectedId, ...]`: populates `rootGroupRef.current` with building geometry

When theme toggles, Effect 1 runs and replaces `rootGroupRef.current` with a new empty group. Effect 2 never re-runs (none of its deps changed), so the building geometry is never added to the new scene. The fix is one line — add `theme` to Effect 2's deps so it repopulates geometry after the renderer rebuilds.

The fix is one line. Here's exactly what was happening:

**Bug**: Theme toggle → Effect 1 (`[theme]`) disposes the old renderer and creates a new scene with a fresh empty `rootGroupRef.current`. Effect 2 (geometry population) has no `theme` dep, so it never re-runs → new scene stays empty → building invisible.

**Fix**: Adding `theme` to Effect 2's deps ensures it fires after Effect 1 completes (React always runs effects top-to-bottom), so it populates the new scene with all the building geometry, now using the freshly-resolved `paintBundleRef.current` that already has the correct dark/light material colors.

content.text:
Now I have everything I need. Let me plan what's wrong and what the fixes are:

**Issues:**
1. **Zoom is linear** (`radius += delta * 0.012`) — feels slow when far, hyper-sensitive when close
2. **No cursor-anchored zoom** — scrolling zooms toward target center, not where cursor is
3. **No wheel delta normalization** — `deltaMode` (line/page) not handled, mouse vs trackpad wildly different
4. **MMB = orbit** (should be pan — Revit/ArchiCAD standard)
5. **No RMB = orbit** (standard in every 3D tool)
6. **No inertia** — orbit snaps dead on release, feels abrupt
7. **Drag threshold too small (2px)** — any tiny click wiggle = accidental orbit

- `onMove`: uses `cumulativeDragPx > DRAG_THRESHOLD_PX` threshold before setting `dragMoved`; tracks `inertiaVx/Vy` on orbit drags.

- Wheel listener changed from `{ passive: true }` to `{ passive: false }`.

content.text:
All changes verified and tests pass. Here's what changed and why each fix matters:

| Problem | Fix |
|---|---|
| Zoom too fast far away, too slow close up | `dolly` now multiplicative (`radius *= exp(δ × 0.001)`) — 10% per mouse notch at any distance |
| Zoom toward viewport center, not cursor | Cursor-anchored zoom: after dolly, `nudgeTarget(ΔR × (rayDir + sphericalDir))` — exact formula that keeps the world point under cursor fixed |
| Horizontal trackpad scroll ignored | `deltaX` → `rig.pan()` for two-finger horizontal swipes |
| `deltaMode` not handled | `wheelDelta` now normalises line (×20) and page (×600) modes |
| RMB didn't orbit | `classifyPointer`: button 2 → `'orbit'`; context menu suppressed on RMB drag |
| MMB was orbit (counterintuitive) | `classifyPointer`: button 1 → `'pan'` (Revit/ArchiCAD standard) |
| Tiny click wiggle = accidental orbit | Cumulative drag threshold: orbit doesn't start until 5px of total movement |
| Orbit snaps dead on release | Inertia: `vx *= 0.87` per frame, stops in ~0.6s naturally |

message.content:
This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation.

Summary:
1. Primary Request and Intent:

The conversation covers multiple feature requests for the `bim-ai` project:

**Completed:**
- Apply the conservative design system from `~/repos/hof-os` to the project, with a Makefile toggle (`make dev design=conservative|default`), defaulting to `conservative`
- Fix conservative design system breaking all UI layouts (missing structural CSS tokens)
- Fix theme toggle (dark/light) making the 3D building disappear
- Overhaul 3D navigation: proportional zoom, cursor-anchored zoom, RMB=orbit, MMB=pan, orbit inertia, drag threshold, deltaMode normalization

**In Progress (most recent, interrupted):**
- Make 2D plan canvas select tool pan the view when clicking/dragging empty space (no shift needed)
- Add interactive zoom control to 2D canvas (clickable scale bar with presets, fit-to-view, min-zoom setting)
- Fix 3D orbit target being stuck at origin (0,0,0) while building is at non-zero world coordinates — user says "it seems fixated to the 0/0/0 part, from where I can rotate and zoom, but not move"

2. Key Technical Concepts:
- CSS custom property cascade (tokens-default.css as structural base, conservative.css as color/font override)
- Vite alias `@bim-ai-design-system.css` resolved from env var `VITE_DESIGN_SYSTEM`
- React two-effect architecture bug (Effect 1 `[theme]` rebuilds renderer; Effect 2 populates geometry — theme dep missing from Effect 2 caused disappearing building)
- Multiplicative (exponential) dolly: `radius *= Math.exp(deltaPx * 0.001)` — zoom rate proportional to distance
- Cursor-anchored zoom formula: `nudge = deltaR * (cursorRayDir + sphericalDir)` — mathematically exact, mirrors 2D anchor zoom
- Orbit inertia: velocity decay (`vx *= 0.87` per frame, ~0.6s stop)
- Three.js orthographic camera for 2D plan, perspective camera for 3D orbit
- `camRef.current.half` = half-viewport-height in meters (zoom level in 2D)
- `rig.frame(box)` + `computeRootBoundingBox` for camera auto-fit

3. Files and Code Sections:

- **`Makefile`**
  - Added `design ?= conservative` variable
  - `dev-web` target: `API_PORT=$(API_PORT) VITE_DESIGN_SYSTEM=$(design) $(PNPM) --filter @bim-ai/web dev ...`
  - Usage: `make dev` (conservative), `make dev design=default`

- **`packages/web/src/design-systems/conservative.css`**
  - Critical fix: import order now provides all structural tokens from default first, then conservative overrides colors/font
  ```css
  @import url('https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Source+Serif+4:ital,opsz,wght@0,8..60,400;0,8..60,600;0,8..60,700;1,8..60,400&display=swap');
  @import '@bim-ai/design-tokens/tokens-default.css';
  @import '@bim-ai/design-tokens/tokens-dark.css';
  @import '@bim-ai/design-tokens/conservative.css';
  html, body, #root { height: 100%; margin: 0; padding: 0; }
  ```

- **`packages/web/src/viewport/cameraRig.ts`**
  - `classifyPointer`: MMB→`'pan'`, RMB (button 2)→`'orbit'`
  - `dolly` now multiplicative: `this.state.radius = clamp(this.state.radius * Math.exp(deltaPx * sensitivity), minRadius, maxRadius)` with `sensitivity = 0.001`
  - New `nudgeTarget(delta: Vec3)` method: `this.state.target = add(this.state.target, delta)`
  - `wheelDelta` updated: deltaMode=1 (line) ×20, deltaMode=2 (page) ×600

- **`packages/web/src/viewport/cameraRig.test.ts`**
  - Updated `'Middle mouse → orbit'` → `'Middle mouse → pan'` (expected `'pan'`)
  - Added `'Right mouse → orbit'`: `expect(classifyPointer({ button: 2 })).toBe('orbit')`
  - Updated `wheelDelta` test: `wheelDelta({ deltaY: 3, deltaMode: 1 })` → `60`

- **`packages/web/src/Viewport.tsx`**
  - Added `theme` to geometry-building effect deps (fixes disappearing building on theme toggle)
  - Navigation vars inside `useEffect([theme])`: `cumulativeDragPx`, `inertiaVx/Vy`, `INERTIA_DECAY = 0.87`, `DRAG_THRESHOLD_PX = 5`
  - `onDown`: resets cumulative drag + kills inertia
  - `onMove`: cumulative threshold before orbit starts; tracks `inertiaVx/Vy`
  - `onWheel` rewrite: `ev.preventDefault()`, NDC-based cursor-anchored zoom via `rig.nudgeTarget()`, deltaMode normalization, horizontal `deltaX` → `rig.pan()`
  - `tick()`: inertia decay block before `renderer.render()`
  - `contextmenu` listener added + cleaned up; wheel listener changed to `{ passive: false }`

- **`packages/web/src/plan/PlanCanvas.tsx`** (read, not yet modified)
  - `onDown` (line 531): Pan only on `(ev.buttons & 2) === 2 || ev.buttons === 4 || ev.shiftKey` — LMB does NOT pan
  - `camRef.current.half` = zoom level (meters); `resizeCam` = useCallback at component level
  - Static scale bar at bottom-left: `pointer-events-none` div showing `━━━ {sb*100} cm`
  - `grp` = THREE.Group inside rendering effect; accessible to `onDown` closure

- **`packages/web/src/Viewport.tsx`** (partially read for pending work)
  - `computeRootBoundingBox` at line 448; called in hotkey handler (lines 813-819) via `rig.frame(box)`
  - Geometry effect ends around line 977: deps `[elementsById, selectedId, viewerCategoryHidden, viewerClipElevMm, viewerClipFloorElevMm, sectionBoxActive, theme]`
  - `cameraRigRef.current` and `cameraRef.current` accessible from geometry effect

4. Errors and Fixes:
- **Conservative design breaking layout**: `--shell-statusbar-height` undefined → AppShell CSS grid rows collapsed → absolute-positioned overlays (ViewCube, Walk buttons) appeared at top. Fixed by importing `tokens-default.css` before `conservative.css`.
- **Building disappears on theme toggle**: Effect 1 `[theme]` rebuilt renderer + new empty `rootGroupRef`. Effect 2 (geometry population) had no `theme` dep so never re-ran. Fixed by adding `theme` to Effect 2's deps.
- **Edit conflict**: On one edit to `Viewport.tsx`, got "File has been modified since read" error — resolved by re-reading before editing.
- **Test failures after cameraRig changes**: `classifyPointer` and `wheelDelta` tests needed updating to match new behavior (MMB→pan, deltaMode normalization). Updated tests accordingly. All 22 tests pass.

5. Problem Solving:
- **Import cascade for design systems**: Established pattern: always import `tokens-default.css` + `tokens-dark.css` first as structural base, then theme-specific overrides on top. This makes any design system work correctly without needing to duplicate structural tokens.
- **Cursor-anchored zoom math**: Derived exact formula `nudge = deltaR * (D + S)` where D = cursor ray unit vector, S = spherical unit vector (target→camera). Verified: at cursor center D≈−S so nudge≈0; at cursor offset, nudge proportional to offset. Matches the 2D `ratio = 1 - oldScale/newScale` approach.
- **3D orbit target at origin**: Building is at world coordinates ~(4, 0, −6.5) (from plan mm coords), but camera rig initializes with `target: { x: 0, y: 1.35, z: 0 }`. Solution: auto-fit on first geometry load using `rig.frame(computeRootBoundingBox(root))`.

6. All User Messages:
- "Does the project have a design system? So I do have in other projects something like conservative.css... Please just copy the conservative CSS design system from the Hoff OS... apply it so that when I restart, make dev, we use the conservative design system. Obviously, I would want to be able to switch this."
- "can you have that it applies the conservative one by default?"
- [Image showing mispositioned UI] "fantastic that works, but A LOT OF ELEMENTS (e.g. the 3d cube to see front/north etc) are totally off. position-wise etc. please look into that carefully and FIX ALL. i need to be able to apply any kind of design system and the UI works perfectly"
- "when i toggle the themes (dark/light), i often don't see the construction anymore. can you check why? and accordingly fix?"
- [Image of 3D view] "the whole navigation of the 2d/3d plane feels SUPER off. it is totally counter-intuivie how to navigate in the 3d area, zooming is terrible, i constantly accidentlly flip the house so to say etc -> how can we create a really outstanding experience for this?"
- "ok, can you have that the make dev has make dev design=conservative or design=default and such? that is a bit easier"
- [Image of 2D plan view] "much better already, but i think we can improve way further; e.g. let me define the scale how close i want to zoom in / how granular i want to work. make it easy to go to the right view options, think about a fantastic UX here as well. moving around the planes should be absolutely intuitive, e.g. if i have the select tool, can i use it to move around as well without shift in case no element is being 'grabbed' at the moment? those kind of things."
- [Sent while agent was working] "it also seems to me i can't really move around in the 3d plane, which is odd. it seems fixated to the 0/0/0 part, from wher ei can rotate and zoom, but not move - is that also the behaviour in revit? act accordingly"

7. Pending Tasks:
- **2D PlanCanvas — select tool = pan**: In `onDown`, when `planTool === 'select'` and LMB down on empty space (raycast hit test finds no element) → start pan drag without needing Shift
- **2D PlanCanvas — space-bar pan**: Hold Space + LMB drag = pan regardless of active tool (standard in Figma, Sketch, Adobe tools)
- **2D PlanCanvas — interactive zoom control**: Replace static `pointer-events-none` scale bar with interactive component: clickable shows preset zoom levels (Close-up 2m, Room 5m, Floor plan 12m, Building 25m, Site 80m), fit-to-view button, min-zoom setting (configurable `minZoomRef`)
- **3D Viewport — auto-fit camera to building**: On first geometry load, call `rig.frame(computeRootBoundingBox(root))` + update THREE camera. Use `hasAutoFittedRef = useRef(false)` to do this only once. This fixes orbit target stuck at origin when building is at non-zero world coordinates.

8. Current Work:
Immediately before this summary request, was in the middle of implementing the navigation and UX improvements requested in the user's latest two messages. The agent had:
1. Done a full audit of `PlanCanvas.tsx` via Explore agent — learned exact line numbers for `onDown` (531), `resizeCam` (useCallback at 284), scale bar (796-798), `camRef` structure (141-148), `halfUi` state (148)
2. Read `onDown` code directly — confirmed pan only triggers on RMB/MMB/Shift, NOT plain LMB
3. Read `resizeCam` — confirmed it's a `useCallback` at component level, accessible from component body
4. Identified `grp` as the THREE.Group accessible within the effect closure (for hit-testing in `onDown`)
5. Was about to implement all four pending tasks above simultaneously

Was in the process of reading `Viewport.tsx` geometry effect end (around line 977) to find the exact insertion point for the auto-fit code, when the summary was requested.

9. Optional Next Step:
Implement the two pending feature groups simultaneously:

**In `packages/web/src/Viewport.tsx`** — add auto-fit to fix "fixated to 0/0/0":
Add `hasAutoFittedRef = useRef(false)` at component level. In the geometry-building effect (deps: `[elementsById, selectedId, viewerCategoryHidden, viewerClipElevMm, viewerClipFloorElevMm, sectionBoxActive, theme]`), after all geometry is built, add:
```js
if (!hasAutoFittedRef.current) {
  const box = computeRootBoundingBox(root);
  if (box && cameraRigRef.current && cameraRef.current) {
    cameraRigRef.current.frame(box);
    const snap = cameraRigRef.current.snapshot();
    cameraRef.current.position.set(snap.position.x, snap.position.y, snap.position.z);
    cameraRef.current.up.set(snap.up.x, snap.up.y, snap.up.z).normalize();
    cameraRef.current.lookAt(snap.target.x, snap.target.y, snap.target.z);
    hasAutoFittedRef.current = true;
  }
}
```

**In `packages/web/src/plan/PlanCanvas.tsx`** — select=pan, space=pan, interactive zoom control:
Modify `onDown` to detect LMB + select tool + no element hit → start pan. Add `spaceDownRef` for space-bar pan. Add `minZoomRef` and `handleFitToView`/`handleZoomTo` callbacks. Replace static scale bar with interactive `ZoomControl` component.

Direct quote from user's most recent message: "it also seems to me i can't really move around in the 3d plane, which is odd. it seems fixated to the 0/0/0 part, from where i can rotate and zoom, but not move - is that also the behaviour in revit? act accordingly"

And from the earlier message: "moving around the planes should be absolutely intuitive, e.g. if i have the select tool, can i use it to move around as well without shift in case no element is being 'grabbed' at the moment?"

If you need specific details from before compaction (like exact code snippets, error messages, or content you generated), read the full transcript at: /Users/jhoetter/.claude/projects/-Users-jhoetter-repos-bim-ai/df0eea30-baa5-4b96-b5f6-eed6ae513c12.jsonl
Continue the conversation from where it left off without asking the user any further questions. Resume directly — do not acknowledge the summary, do not recap what was happening, do not preface with "I'll continue" or similar. Pick up the last task as if the break never happened.

# bim-ai monorepo Makefile — mirrors collaboration-ai ergonomics.

_HOF_OS_IMPORT_DB := $(DATABASE_URL)
_HOF_OS_IMPORT_JWT := $(HOF_SUBAPP_JWT_SECRET)
ifneq ($(and $(strip $(_HOF_OS_IMPORT_JWT)),$(strip $(_HOF_OS_IMPORT_DB))),)
DATABASE_URL := $(_HOF_OS_IMPORT_DB)
HOF_SUBAPP_JWT_SECRET := $(_HOF_OS_IMPORT_JWT)
HOFOS_SUBAPP_NATIVE := 1
endif

PYTHON ?= python3.13
APP_DIR := app
PNPM := pnpm

# Local suite port allocation:
#   2000 → bim-ai web (:8500 API)  <-- this repo (avoids 3xxx used by sister apps)
WEB_PORT ?= 2000
API_PORT ?= 8500
design ?= conservative

.PHONY: help install dev dev-api dev-web kill-ports seed \
	db-up db-down db-reset db-logs \
	test test-py test-js format format-check python-format-check lint architecture \
	typecheck verify build clean

help:
	@echo "bim-ai Makefile"
	@echo "  install   — pnpm + Python venv"
	@echo "  dev       — db-up + API + Web (:$(API_PORT) / :$(WEB_PORT)); make dev design=conservative|default"
	@echo "  verify    — format-check, lint, architecture, tc, pytest, vite build"

install:
	$(PNPM) install
	cd $(APP_DIR) && $(PYTHON) -m venv .venv && .venv/bin/pip install --upgrade pip && .venv/bin/pip install -e ".[dev]"

db-up:
	docker compose -f infra/docker-compose.yml up -d
	@echo "Postgres localhost:5545  Redis localhost:6392  MinIO http://localhost:9120"

db-down:
	docker compose -f infra/docker-compose.yml down

db-reset:
	docker compose -f infra/docker-compose.yml down -v
	$(MAKE) db-up

db-logs:
	docker compose -f infra/docker-compose.yml logs -f --tail=100

kill-ports:
	@PORTS="$(API_PORT) $(WEB_PORT)"; \
	WS_TAG="$(CURDIR)"; \
	for _ in 1 2 3 4 5 6; do \
	  for p in $$PORTS; do \
	    pids=$$(lsof -ti :$$p 2>/dev/null); \
	    [ -n "$$pids" ] && kill -9 $$pids 2>/dev/null || true; \
	  done; \
	  pkill -9 -f "vite.*$$WS_TAG"     2>/dev/null || true; \
	  pkill -9 -f "uvicorn.*$$WS_TAG"   2>/dev/null || true; \
	  pkill -9 -f "concurrently.*$$WS_TAG" 2>/dev/null || true; \
	  pkill -9 -f "turbo run dev"       2>/dev/null || true; \
	  busy=""; \
	  for p in $$PORTS; do \
	    lsof -ti :$$p >/dev/null 2>&1 && busy="$$busy $$p"; \
	  done; \
	  [ -z "$$busy" ] && exit 0; \
	  sleep 0.5; \
	done; \
	echo "kill-ports: still in use after retries:$$busy" >&2; \
	exit 1

ifeq ($(HOFOS_SUBAPP_NATIVE),1)
dev: kill-ports seed
else
dev: db-up kill-ports seed
endif
	@echo "→ API   http://127.0.0.1:$(API_PORT)/api/health"
	@echo "→ Web   http://127.0.0.1:$(WEB_PORT)"
	$(PNPM) -w exec concurrently -k -n api,web -c blue,magenta \
	  "$(MAKE) dev-api" \
	  "$(MAKE) dev-web"

dev-api:
	cd $(APP_DIR) && PYTHONPATH=. .venv/bin/python -m uvicorn bim_ai.main:app --host 127.0.0.1 --port $(API_PORT) --reload

dev-web:
	API_PORT=$(API_PORT) VITE_DESIGN_SYSTEM=$(design) $(PNPM) --filter @bim-ai/web dev --port $(WEB_PORT) --host 127.0.0.1 --strictPort

seed:
	cd $(APP_DIR) && PYTHONPATH=. .venv/bin/python scripts/seed.py

test: test-py test-js

test-py:
	cd $(APP_DIR) && PYTHONPATH=. .venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/ -q

test-js:
	$(PNPM) -w turbo test

format:
	$(PNPM) -w prettier --write "**/*.{ts,tsx,js,jsx,json,md,yml,yaml}"
	cd $(APP_DIR) && .venv/bin/ruff format bim_ai tests scripts

format-check:
	$(PNPM) -w prettier --check "**/*.{ts,tsx,js,jsx,json,md,yml,yaml}"

python-format-check:
	cd $(APP_DIR) && .venv/bin/ruff format --check bim_ai tests scripts

lint:
	$(PNPM) -w eslint "packages/**/*.{ts,tsx}"
	cd $(APP_DIR) && .venv/bin/ruff check bim_ai tests scripts

architecture:
	node scripts/check-architecture.mjs

typecheck:
	$(PNPM) -w turbo typecheck

build:
	$(PNPM) -w turbo build

verify: format-check python-format-check lint architecture typecheck test build
	@echo "verify: PASS"

clean:
	$(PNPM) -w turbo clean || true
	rm -rf node_modules **/node_modules **/dist **/.turbo
	rm -rf $(APP_DIR)/.venv $(APP_DIR)/.pytest_cache
